basics.name: Marcio Merlone
basics.label: Platform Architect & Full-Stack Reliability Engineer
basics.summary: Bridging the gap between ambitious technical vision and reliable production systems through collaborative engineering and operational empathy. Specialist in building resilient, accessible platforms that scale from foundational infrastructure to pixel-perfect UIs for global brands like HP and Akqa.[1, 2]

expertise_pillars.description: Architecting resilient 'Internal Developer Platforms' (IDPs) that reduce developer friction and ensure 99.9% uptime.

expertise_pillars.name: High-Performance UI Architecture
expertise_pillars.description: Scaling global frontend systems for Fortune 500 brands with a focus on 'Accessibility as Architecture'.

expertise_pillars.name: Operational Stewardship
expertise_pillars.description: A 14-year track record of 'living with decisions,' evolving mission-critical legacy ERPs into scalable cloud-ready environments.

highlights: Developed high-performance component libraries using React and TypeScript, reducing p95 latency and improving SEO for global brand platforms.
Collaborated across cross-functional 'Agile' teams to translate complex UX requirements into production-ready interaction patterns.

work.position: Director of Platform Infrastructure & ERP Stewardship
highlights: Modernized a mission-critical PHP-based Project Management ERP through iterative refactoring, maintaining zero-downtime over a 14-year lifecycle.
Hardened global enterprise security via pfSense firewalls and zero-trust VLAN segmentation, effectively eliminating production threats.

case_studies.decision: Leveraged ESXi virtualization and automated snapshots to create a self-healing private cloud environment.
case_studies.outcome: Achieved 99.99% infrastructure availability and enabled instant environment replication for dev/test teams.

case_studies.title: Accessibility as Performance
case_studies.context: Scaling frontend UIs for HP required high-performance, inclusive design.[4]
case_studies.decision: Implemented 'Accessibility as Code' using semantic HTML and rigorous ARIA testing before it reached production.
case_studies.outcome: Reduced manual QA time by 30% and improved Lighthouse accessibility scores to 100/100 across client properties.
